title: Linux hacked onto a PS5 to turn Sony's console into a Steam Machine GTA V Enhanced Edition runs at 60 FPS on 1440pwith ray tracing
summary: Andy Nguyen ported Linux to the PS5, turning it into a PC, and successfully ran GTA V on Steam at 60 FPS on 1440p with ray tracing.
insight: Hackers can repurpose PS5 as PC, preserving gaming legacy and options.

title: Lock in the RTX 5070 for $599 before prices skyrocket even further custom overclocked model is only $50 above MSRP
summary: The RTX 5070 provides strong 1440p performance with 12GB of GDDR7 memory, supporting Nvidia's latest features like DLSS 4 and Reflex 2.
insight: Secure a powerful GPU at competitive pricing before prices surge higher.

title: Valve changes Steam Machine release date to this 'year,' second change as AI-fueled memory and shortage crisis deepens official announcements went from "early 2026" to "first half of 2026" to "this year"
summary: Valve has shifted the Steam Machine release date to 'this year', marking its second update in 2026. Initially, the console was expected to arrive in 'early 2026', but was later revised to the 'first half of 2026'. This latest announcement brings the release window to an unspecified period this year.
insight: Steam Machine's release timeline remains unpredictable due to ongoing shortages

title: Nintendo sues the US government over tariffs Japanese videogame giant seeks 200 billion refund with interest
summary: Nintendo of America sues the US government for 200 billion refund, citing tariffs from Trump's executive orders.
insight: Nintendo's lawsuit challenges Trump-era tariffs, impacting gaming industry worldwide.

title: AMD VP uses AI to create Radeon Linux userland driver in Python senior AI engineer says he "didn't open the editor once"
summary: AMD's VP used AI to create a Linux driver in Python, but it's for testing only.
insight: AI code generation impacts traditional software development processes and expertise.

title: Engineer receives $30,000 for exposing a vulnerability affecting 7,000 robot vacuum cleaners tinkerer just wanted to drive his robot vacuum with a PS5 controller
summary: A software engineer discovered a vulnerability in 7,000 robot vacuum cleaners while experimenting with gamepad control. He received $30,000 from DJI for his find. The engineer's initial goal was to use a PS5 controller to control his robot vacuum.
insight: Consumer device security vulnerabilities can have significant monetary rewards.

title: Oracle reportedly set to axe thousands of jobs and freeze hiring as AI data center bets ignite financial perfect storm cuts in cloud division to be backfilled with AI
summary: Oracle is reportedly planning to cut thousands of jobs and freeze hiring in its cloud division, citing significant losses due to AI data center investments.
insight: Oracle's AI strategy is sparking a massive workforce overhaul.

title: Scientists attempt to link 3D printed ghost guns to specific filament brands with chemical fingerprinting major filament makers often white-label products, complicating efforts
summary: Researchers attempt to link 3D printed ghost guns to specific filament brands through chemical analysis, but major filament makers often white-label products, complicating efforts.
insight: Tracing ghost guns through filament proves challenging due to white-labeling.

title: Claude Code deletes developers' production setup, including its database and snapshots 2.5 years of records were nuked in an instant
summary: A developer's over-eager use of Claude Code Terraform resulted in the deletion of the production setup, including the database and its snapshots, leading to the loss of 2.5 years of records.
insight: Careless use of automation tools can lead to devastating data losses.

title: Nvidia CEO Huang declares I love constraints amid ongoing component shortage claims lack of options forces AI clients to only choose the very best
summary: Nvidia CEO Huang says 'I love constraints' due to component shortages, benefiting the company as clients opt for reliable and high-end options, which ultimately hurts regular consumers.
insight: Component shortages limit consumer options, benefiting Nvidia at their expense.

title: This shark PC case will take a $5,499 megabyte out of your pocket Cooler Master Shark X finally migrates to U.S. waters
summary: The Cooler Master Shark X mini-ITX case is now available in the U.S. for $5,499.99, offering a unique design for PC enthusiasts.
insight: Unique shark-shaped case design for PC enthusiasts emerges in the market.

title: Iranian drone strikes hit three AWS data centers in the UAE and Bahrain Iran confirms it targeted Amazon cloud infrastructure
summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps struck three AWS data centers in the UAE and Bahrain, targeting Amazon's cloud infrastructure. The Bahrain facility was specifically targeted because it hosts U.S. military workloads. This is a significant escalation in the region's cyber and physical conflict.
insight: Amazon's cloud infrastructure is now a critical target in global conflicts

title: Apple's 18-core M5 Max destroys 96-core Ryzen Threadripper Pro 9995WX in Geekbench GPU performance is much less impressive
summary: Apple's M5 Max processor outperforms a 96-core Ryzen Threadripper in multi-thread workloads, but its GPU performance is less impressive.
insight: Intellectual property and architecture advancements can greatly impact processor performance.

title: PC processors entered the Gigahertz era today in the year 2000 with AMD's Athlon AMD hit marketing gold with its 1 GHz Athlon, beat Intel by a nose
summary: AMD shipped the 1 GHz Athlon CPU on March 6, 2000, marking the start of the Gigahertz era in PC processors.
insight: AMD beat Intel to 1 GHz, gaining a significant marketing advantage.

title: Pironman 5 Pro Max Raspberry Pi 5 case gains 4.3-inch touchscreen, camera mount, speakers, microphone, and more
summary: The Pironman 5 Pro Max Raspberry Pi 5 case now features a 4.3-inch touchscreen, camera mount, and more. This new case is compatible with the Raspberry Pi 5 and similar single-board computers. It offers enhanced features and storage options.
insight: Enhanced Raspberry Pi 5 experience with multimedia features and storage options

title: GEEKOM A5 Pro 2026 Edition Review Part 3: Ubuntu 25.10 on an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U mini PC
summary: This review tests the GEEKOM A5 Pro 2026 Edition mini PC with Ubuntu 25.10, covering system performance, benchmarks, storage, and networking. The AMD Ryzen 5 7530U mini PC is put through various workloads to measure CPU temperature, fan noise, and power consumption. The results showcase how the mini PC performs under Linux.
insight: Linux performance on AMD Ryzen 5 7530U mini PCs is promising and efficient.

title: Mozilla says Claude AI uncovered over 100 Firefox bugs in just two weeks, including 14 high-severity flaws
summary: Mozilla partnered with Anthropic's AI to identify and patch Firefox vulnerabilities, uncovering over 100 bugs in two weeks, including 14 high-severity flaws.
insight: AI-assisted bug-hunting enhances security by uncovering vulnerabilities in a short time frame.

title: Modder turns a PS5 into a Linux Steam Machine, runs GTA V with ray tracing
summary: A modder, theflow0, transformed a PS5 into a Linux gaming PC capable of running GTA V with ray tracing at 1440p and 60 FPS.
insight: PS5's hardware can be repurposed for Linux gaming, bypassing Sony's ecosystem restrictions.

title: At MWC 2026, the telecom industry pivoted from 5G to AI-powered 6G
summary: The telecom industry shifted focus from 5G to AI-powered 6G at MWC 2026.
insight: AI-powered 6G promises revolutionary wireless connectivity and enhanced user experiences.

title: Arc Raiders bug was saving Discord private chats and login tokens in plaintext files
summary: Arc Raiders' Discord SDK logged and saved user data in plaintext files, prompting Embark Studios to issue a hotfix and disable Discord logging for further investigation.
insight: Private user data was exposed due to inadequate data protection measures.
